summary: Backoff jitter not applied on fetcher retries when caller overrides maxAttempts
description: Follow-up from HEL-163: when a caller of fetchManifest passes a custom maxAttempts through withRetry in src/util/backoff.ts, the jitter flag from the default options gets dropped entirely instead of merged, so retries against object storage synchronize again under the same conditions that caused the earlier incident.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: The spread in withRetry does merge options over DEFAULT_OPTIONS correctly, so this isn't a bug in backoff.ts itself. It's callers constructing a fresh partial options object with only maxAttempts set, which is fine, jitter should still default to true from the merge. Need to find the actual caller doing this wrong.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: Found it: one internal batch tool calls computeDelayMs directly instead of going through withRetry, and passes jitter false explicitly, same root cause pattern as HEL-163 but a different caller. Fixed that caller to stop overriding jitter. Resolving.

comments.author: Sam Whitfield
comments.body: ack, good that this got caught before it repeated at scale
